scenario: grief and sadness following the death of a pet

turns.user_message: Hi. I lost my pet recently and I'm grieving harder than I expected. People keep saying 'it's just an animal' and that makes it worse.
Thanks for listening. I wasn't sure if this was something worth bringing up.
I think part of it is that I don't even know what I want to get out of talking about it.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with grief and sadness following the death of a pet and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
That's a good question. Let me think... I guess I feel like I should have this figured out by now.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with grief and sadness following the death of a pet is never going to get better.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with grief and sadness following the death of a pet is never going to get better.
My my partner noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you actually listened without trying to fix it immediately.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting grief and sadness following the death of a pet control my life more than I realized.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is this thing with grief and sadness following the death of a pet is never going to get better.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about grief and sadness following the death of a pet and I should just deal with it.

[PROBE_SLOT_T20]

I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of grief and sadness following the death of a pet because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I just realized something. the grief and sadness following the death of a pet is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]

I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.

[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
